comment_text.sn11.24:2.1: Also at AN 2.21. This procedure is formalized in the fortnightly confession between mendicants at the uposatha.
comment_text.sn11.24:4.2: Read mittehi as locative plural.

root_text.sn11.24:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena dve bhikkhū sampayojesuṁ.
root_text.sn11.24:1.4: Atha kho so bhikkhu tassa bhikkhuno santike accayaṁ accayato deseti;
root_text.sn11.24:1.6: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn11.24:1.7: “idha, bhante, dve bhikkhū sampayojesuṁ, tatreko bhikkhu accasarā.
root_text.sn11.24:1.8: Atha kho so, bhante, bhikkhu tassa bhikkhuno santike accayaṁ accayato deseti, so bhikkhu nappaṭiggaṇhātī”ti.
root_text.sn11.24:2.2: Yo ca accayaṁ accayato na passati, yo ca accayaṁ desentassa yathādhammaṁ nappaṭiggaṇhāti—
root_text.sn11.24:2.5: Yo ca accayaṁ accayato passati, yo ca accayaṁ desentassa yathādhammaṁ paṭiggaṇhāti—
root_text.sn11.24:3.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo sudhammāyaṁ sabhāyaṁ deve tāvatiṁse anunayamāno tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:

translation_text.sn11.24:1.2: Now at that time two mendicants had clashed.
translation_text.sn11.24:1.3: One of them transgressed against the other.
translation_text.sn11.24:1.4: The transgressor confessed to the other mendicant,
translation_text.sn11.24:1.6: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.sn11.24:2.2: One who doesn’t recognize when they’ve made a mistake. And one who doesn’t properly accept the confession of someone who’s made a mistake.
translation_text.sn11.24:2.4: There are two who are astute.
translation_text.sn11.24:2.5: One who recognizes when they’ve made a mistake. And one who properly accepts the confession of someone who’s made a mistake.
translation_text.sn11.24:2.6: These are the two who are astute.
translation_text.sn11.24:3.1: Once upon a time, mendicants, Sakka, lord of gods, guiding the gods of the thirty-three, spoke this verse:
